detail: It is time to make the icl-diversity project camera-ready! It was accepted to the Human-AI Co-Creativity @ ICML 2026! The workshop said a week ago that they would share further instructions about camera-ready papers soon, but I want to post the paper on arxiv so I can share it. Please start by doing research on what needs to be done.…

It is time to make the icl-diversity project camera-ready! It was accepted to the Human-AI Co-Creativity @ ICML 2026! The workshop said a week ago that they would share further instructions about camera-ready papers soon, but I want to post the paper on arxiv so I can share it. Please start by doing research on what needs to be done.…

Researched ICML-2026-workshop camera-ready + NeurIPS-2026 dual-submission/preprint + arXiv requirements + reviewer-addition norms, red-teamed the approach, and produced a phased camera-ready/arXiv plan and a `/camera-ready-paper` skill design; wrote 3 deferred-work GitHub-issue files to `icl-diversity/camera-ready-issues/`. Key finding: posting the accepted (non-archival) workshop version to arXiv now is policy-safe for the NeurIPS submission, provided no NeurIPS strings leak and no archival resubmission happens before 24 Sep 2026. Awaiting go-ahead to write the skill and/or execute the arXiv-v1 track.

— your `../results/...` paths and `\bibliography{refs}` must be flattened and shipped with the `.bbl`. Real work, not a detail.
  4. **Identity leaks beyond visible text** — scan the built PDF's metadata + raw bytes for `neurips`, `under review`, `4open`, `anonymous`, and the build path `cs29824/matthew`.

### Execution plan (pending your go-ahead — no paper edits made yet)

**Immediate track → arXiv v1 (the accepted version):**
- **Phase 0 · Pre-flight** — sanity-check your arXiv endorsement covers cs.CL/cs.LG; pick categories (primary **cs.CL**, cross-list **cs.LG**/**cs.AI**).
- **Phase 1 · De-anonymize** — branch off `anon-icml-workshop`; real author block + acknowledgments + funding; swap `anonymous.4open.science/...` → `github.com/AMindToThink/icl-diversity`; **no PMLR banner**; rebuild.
- **Phase 2 · Integrity gate** — `build_bib.py` → `verify_cites.py` → `build_paper_macros.py` → `pytest tests/test_paper_macros.py`; grep for hand-typed numbers; PDF metadata/identity scan.
- **Phase 3 · arXiv package & submit v1** — flatten into one dir (copy `paper_macros.tex`, tables, all figures; rewrite paths to filenames; ship `.bbl`; add `\pdfoutput=1`); compile standalone clean; **license = arXiv non-exclusive** (conservative, and irrevocable per version); Comments = "ICML 2026 Workshop on Human-AI Co-Creativity (non-archival)" with **zero NeurIPS strings**; submit.

**Deferred track →** the three issue files above (workshop response, then v2 experiments).

---

# `/camera-ready-paper` skill design (general & reusable)

Location per your convention: **`~/.claude/skills/camera-ready-paper/SKILL.md`** (+ a `references/` folder). It encodes the *process*, pointing at each project's specifics rather than hardcoding icl-diversity:

1. **Intake** — venue + **archival status**, any concurrent submissions and their venues, deadlines, template, and where the repo's numbers/bib come from.
2. **Dual-submission safety check** — fetch each concurrent venue's policy; classify archival vs non-archival; emit a go/no-go + guardrails (no in-review-venue strings, no aggressive advertising, no archival resubmission while under review).
3. **De-anonymization checklist** — authors/affiliations/emails, acks, funding, repo URLs, banner decision, strip identity metadata.
4. **Integrity gate** — numbers-from-scripts + bib-from-IDs + unit tests + hand-typed-number grep + PDF metadata scan (your house rules, generalized).
5. **arXiv packaging** — endorsement check, flat-bundle recipe, license guidance, categories/comments, standalone-compile test, **v1-as-reviewed / v2-extended** versioning.
6. **Reviewer-addition flagging** — footnote + "changes since reviewed" note + arXiv changelog; new numbers still macro-sourced.
7. **Guardrails** — git-identity check, don't-push-without-asking, ask-before-squeezing page limits, fail-loud on integrity violations.
- **`references/policy-quick-reference.md`** — the NeurIPS/arXiv/ICML facts + source URLs gathered today, so the skill doesn't re-research from scratch.

---

I've **not touched the paper or written the skill yet** — that's the next phase and I'll wait for your go-ahead. When you're ready, tell me which to start with:
- **(A)** write this up as a committed design spec + build the `/camera-ready-paper` skill, and/or
- **(B)** begin execution of the arXiv-v1 track (de-anon → integrity gate → flat bundle).

result: Researched ICML-2026-workshop camera-ready + NeurIPS-2026 dual-submission/preprint + arXiv requirements + reviewer-addition norms, red-teamed the approach, and produced a phased camera-ready/arXiv plan and a `/camera-ready-paper` skill design; wrote 3 deferred-work GitHub-issue files to `icl-diversity/camera-ready-issues/`. Key finding: posting the accepted (non-archival) workshop version to arXiv now is policy-safe for the NeurIPS submission, provided no NeurIPS strings leak and no archival resubmission happens before 24 Sep 2026. Awaiting go-ahead to write the skill and/or execute the arXiv-v1 track.

detail: Can we flatten programatically instead of manually?…

I don't like the idea of writing our own arXiv flatten. Is there something we can import instead to do it for us? After merging with main, please start by handling the citations and ensuring the resulting paper is byte identical with the icml anonymous version.
Ok, looks good. Remember: *NO* changes to the content of the paper, which was just accepted to the workshop.

De-anonymized the accepted icl-diversity workshop paper on `main` (4 local commits, byte-identity to the accepted PDF verified, only author/venue metadata changed), built and verified a flat arXiv tarball via arxiv-collector (`arxiv-build/`, 28pp, 0 undefined refs, no NeurIPS/anon strings), and created the reusable `/camera-ready-paper` skill. Remaining are two user actions: fill the Acknowledgements stub (then I regenerate the tarball) and upload to arXiv per `SUBMIT_CHECKLIST.md`; nothing pushed.

The arXiv tarball is **flat, self-contained, and compiles exactly like arXiv will** (28 pp, 0 undefined refs, 0 missing files, real authors, zero NeurIPS/anon strings). Built with the imported `arxiv-collector` + a 2-line `../`-strip — no hand-rolled flattener.

**Your one action before upload:** write the Acknowledgements in `paper/sections/acknowledgements_workshop.tex` (it's an empty stub — the PDF currently shows a bare "Acknowledgements" heading). Then I regenerate the tarball in one step.

Now finishing the approved plan — **Phase 6: the reusable `/camera-ready-paper` skill**, encoding everything this session taught us:

Now the policy quick-reference that backs the skill (with dates so future use knows to re-verify):

The skill is registered (it's now in the available-skills list). Running a final sanity check across everything before I wrap up — skill structure, git state, and the deliverable bundle:

Final sanity check passes: skill is registered with valid frontmatter (SKILL.md + references), git is fully committed and clean with the `backup/pre-camera-ready-main` safety branch intact at `7962049`, the bundle + checklist are in place, and the macro integrity tests are 6/6 green.

## Done — full plan executed

**Research & planning** (earlier): camera-ready requirements, NeurIPS-2026 dual-submission/preprint safety, arXiv mechanics, and reviewer-addition norms — all consolidated; the deferred work is in the 3 GitHub issues you filed.

**Execution on `icl-diversity` `main`** (4 commits, all local — *not pushed*):
- **Established the truth:** your submitted paper is the `anon-icml-workshop` version (it has Peeperkorn 2024 + the metric→approach reframe + expanded Table 1), **not** `main`'s old tip. Merged that in and **proved the compiled paper byte-identical to your accepted PDF** before changing anything.
- **De-anonymized with zero content change:** real authors (Matthew Khoriaty & David Williams-King — ERA Fellowship; Shi Feng — GWU; corresponding `matthewkhoriaty@gmail.com`), `[preprint]` mode (real authors, **no false PMLR copyright banner**), public GitHub URL, empty Acknowledgements stub, and scrubbed the `main_neurips.tex` comment leak. Verified: the only diffs vs your accepted paper are author block / footer / URL / acks heading.
- **arXiv bundle** built with imported `arxiv-collector` (+ a 2-line `../`-strip, not a hand-rolled flattener); compiles standalone exactly like arXiv: **28 pp, 0 undefined refs, 0 missing files, no NeurIPS/anon strings.**

**Skill:** `/camera-ready-paper` created at `~/.claude/skills/camera-ready-paper/` — encodes the safety check, byte-identity gate, the ICML `[preprint]` gotcha, the arxiv-collector+normalize recipe, and the post-review-addition flagging, with a dated policy quick-reference. (It's the natural inverse of your existing `make-anonymous-branch` skill.)

## Your two remaining actions
1. **Write the Acknowledgements** in `paper/sections/acknowledgements_workshop.tex` (empty stub now). Tell me when done and I'll regenerate the final tarball in one step.
2. **Upload to arXiv** using `arxiv-build/SUBMIT_CHECKLIST.md` (primary cs.CL, cross-list cs.LG/cs.AI, arXiv non-exclusive license, Comments = the workshop name, no NeurIPS).

When you're ready I can also `git push` (you said ask first). Deferred: workshop camera-ready (awaiting organizers) and arXiv v2 (reviewer experiments) — tracked in your GitHub issues.

result: De-anonymized the accepted icl-diversity workshop paper on `main` (4 local commits, byte-identity to the accepted PDF verified, only author/venue metadata changed), built and verified a flat arXiv tarball via arxiv-collector (`arxiv-build/`, 28pp, 0 undefined refs, no NeurIPS/anon strings), and created the reusable `/camera-ready-paper` skill. Remaining are two user actions: fill the Acknowledgements stub (then I regenerate the tarball) and upload to arXiv per `SUBMIT_CHECKLIST.md`; nothing pushed.
